channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18569] sanity-sec: timeout - clients lost connection to MGS
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Marc Vef <mvef@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/a1bdbb1f-684a-44dd-9683-4a52e4eaaf4c

test_31 failed with the following error:

Timeout occurred after 445 minutes, last suite running was sanity-sec

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/109661 - 5.15.0-94-generic
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/109661 - 4.18.0-553.27.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

Both clients (vm1 and vm2) lost connection after mounting:

[21607.508267] Lustre: Mounted lustre-client
[21607.835897] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: mount | grep /mnt/lustre' '
[21629.086605] Lustre: 79408:0:(client.c:2358:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) @@@ Request sent has timed out for sent delay: [sent 1734413476/real 0]  req@ffff9c605474f740 x1818664259031424/t0(0) o400->lustre-OST0002-osc-ffff9c6049741800@10.240.22.182@tcp:28/4 lens 224/224 e 0 to 1 dl 1734413492 ref 2 fl Rpc:XNr/200/ffffffff rc 0/-1 job:'' uid:0 gid:0
[21629.086637] Lustre: lustre-MDT0000-mdc-ffff9c6049741800: Connection to lustre-MDT0000 (at 10.240.22.189@tcp) was lost; in progress operations using this service will wait for recovery to complete
[21629.091974] Lustre: 79408:0:(client.c:2358:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) Skipped 1 previous similar message
[21629.097039] LustreError: MGC10.240.22.189@tcp: Connection to MGS (at 10.240.22.189@tcp) was lost; in progress operations using this service will fail
[21634.206602] Lustre: 79408:0:(client.c:2358:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) @@@ Request sent has timed out for sent delay: [sent 1734413481/real 0]  req@ffff9c605474e700 x1818664259032448/t0(0) o400->lustre-OST0001-osc-ffff9c6049741800@10.240.22.182@tcp:28/4 lens 224/224 e 0 to 1 dl 1734413497 ref 2 fl Rpc:XNr/200/ffffffff rc 0/-1 job:'' uid:0 gid:0
[21634.212144] Lustre: 79408:0:(client.c:2358:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) Skipped 8 previous similar messages
[21639.326639] Lustre: 79408:0:(client.c:2358:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) @@@ Request sent has timed out for sent delay: [sent 1734413486/real 0]  req@ffff9c6054447a80 x1818664259034240/t0(0) o400->lustre-OST0006-osc-ffff9c6049741800@10.240.22.182@tcp:28/4 lens 224/224 e 0 to 1 dl 1734413502 ref 2 fl Rpc:XNr/200/ffffffff rc 0/-1 job:'' uid:0 gid:0
[21639.332231] Lustre: 79408:0:(client.c:2358:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) Skipped 4 previous similar messages
[21644.446523] Lustre: 79407:0:(client.c:2358:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) @@@ Request sent has timed out for sent delay: [sent 1734413492/real 0]  req@ffff9c6053e87a80 x1818664259035776/t0(0) o400->lustre-OST0000-osc-ffff9c6049741800@10.240.22.182@tcp:28/4 lens 224/224 e 0 to 1 dl 1734413508 ref 2 fl Rpc:XNr/200/ffffffff rc 0/-1 job:'' uid:0 gid:0
[21644.452090] Lustre: 79407:0:(client.c:2358:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) Skipped 10 previous similar messages
[21797.277618] nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, timed out
[21831.069446] LNet: 2 peer NIs in recovery (showing 2): 10.240.22.189@tcp, 10.240.22.182@tcp
[21831.071116] Lustre: 79408:0:(client.c:2358:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) @@@ Request sent has failed due to network error: [sent 1734413486/real 1734413694]  req@ffff9c6054445380 x1818664259033216/t0(0) o400->MGC10.240.22.189@tcp@10.240.22.189@tcp:26/25 lens 224/224 e 0 to 1 dl 1734413502 ref 1 fl Rpc:EeXNQU/200/ffffffff rc -5/-1 job:'' uid:0 gid:0
[21831.076441] Lustre: 79408:0:(client.c:2358:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) Skipped 9 previous similar messages
[21831.077739] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.22.189@tcp at host 10.240.22.189:7988
[21832.093479] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.22.189@tcp at host 10.240.22.189:7988
[21832.095303] LNetError: Skipped 2 previous similar messages

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
sanity-sec test_31 - Timeout occurred after 445 minutes, last suite running was sanity-sec
item.summary: sanity-sec: timeout - clients lost connection to MGS

status.description: This issue was once resolved, but the resolution was deemed incorrect. From here issues are either marked assigned or resolved.

comments.comment: Possibly, yes. I will need to check those other timeout failures in more detail first. I'll keep you updated
Thanks Marc for keeping an eye on this. If the timeouts are different now, do you want to close this ticket and open a new one if necessary?

Update: Based on timeouts from the last couple of months, it appears the patches from this ticket had an impact, and there are significantly fewer failures since October last year. test_failures_Jan_25-Jan_26.pdf shows the change.

There are still timeouts that need to be further investigated, but they look different than before, so something else could have affected the change in failures also?

[ 2603.114807] LustreError: lustre-MDT0000-mdc-ff485b8585ac6800: operation ldlm_enqueue to node 10.240.46.173@tcp failed: rc = -107
[ 2603.116649] Lustre: lustre-MDT0000-mdc-ff485b8585ac6800: Connection to lustre-MDT0000 (at 10.240.46.173@tcp) was lost; in progress operations using this service will wait for recovery to complete
[ 2605.491269] Lustre: lustre-OST0000-osc-ff485b8585ac6800: Connection to lustre-OST0000 (at 10.240.46.172@tcp) was lost; in progress operations using this service will wait for recovery to complete
[ 2637.234424] Lustre: 156639:0:(client.c:2472:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) @@@ Request sent has timed out for slow reply: [sent 1767331722/real 1767331722]  req@ff485b8589eb1380 x1853180470761856/t0(0) o400->MGC10.240.46.173@tcp@10.240.46.173@tcp:26/25 lens 224/224 e 0 to 1 dl 1767331738 ref 1 fl Rpc:XNQr/200/ffffffff rc 0/-1 job:'kworker.0' uid:0 gid:0 projid:4294967295
[ 2637.239245] LustreError: MGC10.240.46.173@tcp: Connection to MGS (at 10.240.46.173@tcp) was lost; in progress operations using this service will fail
[ 2855.097109] Autotest: Test running for 45 minutes (lustre-reviews_review-ldiskfs-ubuntu_120092.61)
[ 3155.527378] Autotest: Test running for 50 minutes (lustre-reviews_review-ldiskfs-ubuntu_120092.61)
ok - thanks Marc!

Hi pjo, unfortunately, this issue is not fixed yet. Just this month, there were still 30+ timeouts for tests associated with this ticket (plus more untagged timeouts). The goal of the landed patches is to reduce the failure frequency to gain a better understanding of this network-related issue.

I'm reopening this and will let you know when there is a solution.
Merged for 2.17

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61854/
Subject: LU-18569 tests: sanity-sec remove duplicate wait_nm_sync calls
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: e20cc3d570740507de20d4c470ea3bcb7c375082

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61583/
Subject: LU-18569 tests: skip ls for ubuntu clients on sanity-sec/75a
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 5cfde51caa46ac68ce49dff099cda25c7f0d72e8

sanity-sec test 14, sanity-flr test 200a and mmp hit the same issue on Ubuntu 22.04 client on master branch:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/02df93f7-e0df-4020-8a60-f7a7d96e8471
https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/207206cb-d8ce-4708-aa8d-a8f9e070ac13
https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/93d2ec54-1816-4d50-86f2-33cd48f9be7a
https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/81109340-4ea1-432f-a131-6f37cc32ec70
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/64c8b1d1-85f0-43c8-927d-e4251053744d

"Marc Vef <mvef@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61854
Subject: LU-18569 tests: sanity-sec remove duplicate wait_nm_sync calls
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: db3e22abb0415d4c1b484955872dfe2076a65b50

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61353/
Subject: LU-18569 tests: cleanup zconf_mount_clients
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: d63856c657f16cc612efadd3f19266a847c17153

I've been looking at some timeout statistics from the past nine months for Ubuntu 2204 clients; see the attached plots for anyone interested. As expected, the trend appears to be worsening. Notably, two of the top failure contributors, tests sanity-sec/31 and sanity-sec/62, haven't been touched in that time frame. Still, the timeout frequency is not consistently increasing over time (there are also bursts and periods of reduced frequency).

test_failures_Jan-Sep_25.pdf

The logs indicate that the timeout occurs often when running commands such as {ls -l}, {sync}, and {zconf_mount}. I'm looking into reducing the sanity-sec/75a timeouts and also brute forcing the issue, so it can be reproduced consistently.

"Marc Vef <mvef@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61583
Subject: LU-18569 tests: skip ls for ubuntu clients on sanity-sec/75a
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: c2b87ad2037aea8e226a2c09803de6f5a60787db

"Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61372
Subject: LU-18569 dbg: do not unmount in zconf_mount_clients
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 551d115ca43145673caaa5ab286410e84f5440a4

+1 on Master https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/52508c8a-a008-4899-96b2-93d464b03230

Failed TC: sanity-sec/75a
Regression: No / Not determine
Reproducible: No (Mostly passes on rerun)

Client Kernel version    5.15.0-142-generic
Server Kernel version    4.18.0-553.71.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

client log:

...
[21803.375323] nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, still trying^M         
[21803.375380] nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, timed out^M            
[21812.143052] LNet: 2 peer NIs in recovery (showing 2): 10.240.24.50@tcp, 10.240.24.53@tcp^M
[21812.144817] Lustre: 1121431:0:(client.c:2458:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) @@@ Request sent has failed due to network error: [sent 1758154259/real 1758154452]  req@ffff8b5909aa2700 x      1843557076257920/t0(0) o400->MGC10.240.24.53@tcp@10.240.24.53@tcp:26/25 lens 224/224 e 0 to 1 dl 1758154275 ref 1 fl Rpc:EeXNQU/200/ffffffff rc -5/-1 job:'kworker.0' uid:0 gid:0 projid      :4294967295^M
[21812.150827] Lustre: 1121431:0:(client.c:2458:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) Skipped 11 previous similar messages^M
[21812.151443] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.24.53@tcp at host 10.240.24.53:7988^M
[21813.167011] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.24.50@tcp at host 10.240.24.50:7988^M
...

"Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61353
Subject: LU-18569 tests: cleanup zconf_mount_clients
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: ad3df16295a16a8caf737279373c26ac32a8cd06
well, most of our recovery tests restart clients/servers very frequently, but those old tests are doing fine.

However, it seems like sanity-sec is hitting this issue more often than other subtests, which makes me wonder if it is doing something with the network interfaces that is triggering this more often?

It appears that a handful of tests have a significantly higher failure rate than others, like 27a, 27ab, 31, 54, and 62. They all have in common that they restart the clients/servers at some point, and these timeouts seem to occur more frequently during a restarting task, e.g., unmounting or mounting. Sanity-sec test 54 is also highly represented in the failures for LU-18693. Sanity-sec restarts clients fairly often to apply nodemap changes, for example. While, like other tests, random timeouts can occur anywhere, the number of restarts/remounts could be a symptom of why sanity-sec is overrepresented for this issue.

And, the issue seemingly only occurs for Ubuntu clients (mainly 22.04)
Same report is also updated under LU-18693.

I've been using LU-18693 for the "nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, still trying" issue when I see a random timed out test session and find it in the logs. However, it seems like sanity-sec is hitting this issue more often than other subtests, which makes me wonder if it is doing something with the network interfaces that is triggering this more often?

+1 Master https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/3426b751-7a40-4601-85c2-3ad280d899b9

client:  5.15.0-142-generic
Server:  4.18.0-553.53.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

Client log (10.240.27.184)

[21791.916712] nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, still trying^M         
[21791.916741] nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, still trying^M         
[21813.420585] LNet: 2 peer NIs in recovery (showing 2): 10.240.27.192@tcp, 10.240.27.186@tcp^M
[21813.422233] Lustre: 1054975:0:(client.c:2447:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) @@@ Request sent has failed due to network error: [sent 1751553348/real 1751553536]  req@ffff9a538ab02700 x      1836635514129920/t0(0) o400->MGC10.240.27.192@tcp@10.240.27.192@tcp:26/25 lens 224/224 e 0 to 1 dl 1751553364 ref 1 fl Rpc:EeXNQU/200/ffffffff rc -5/-1 job:'kworker.0' uid:0 gid:0 proj      id:4294967295^M
[21813.428204] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.27.192@tcp at host 10.240.27.192:7988^M
[21814.444574] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.27.192@tcp at host 10.240.27.192:7988^M

MDT

21638.716775] LNet: Timeout error while writing to 10.240.27.184:1021. Closing socket: rc = -110^M
[21701.597499] Lustre: lustre-MDT0000: haven't heard from client 3b2ebc9a-b2cf-458c-b92e-9388e6403003 (at 10.240.27.184@tcp) in 101 seconds. I think it's dead, and I am evicting it. ex      p ffff9b887a3d2800, cur 1751553428 deadline 1751553427 last 1751553327^M
[21706.717635] Lustre: lustre-MDT0000: haven't heard from client cd1f08cf-b9d8-4971-997a-2d05d1ce6219 (at 10.240.27.185@tcp) in 105 seconds. I think it's dead, and I am evicting it. ex      p ffff9b886ef6a800, cur 1751553433 deadline 1751553428 last 1751553328^M

OST

[21702.393354] Lustre: lustre-OST0005: haven't heard from client cd1f08cf-b9d8-4971-997a-2d05d1ce6219 (at 10.240.27.185@tcp) in 101 seconds. I think it's dead, and I am evicting it. ex      p ffff9d0c968ff000, cur 1751553429 deadline 1751553428 last 1751553328^M
[21702.905333] Lustre: lustre-OST0006: haven't heard from client cd1f08cf-b9d8-4971-997a-2d05d1ce6219 (at 10.240.27.185@tcp) in 101 seconds. I think it's dead, and I am evicting it. ex      p ffff9d0c836ae800, cur 1751553429 deadline 1751553428 last 1751553328^M
[21702.909148] Lustre: Skipped 1 previous similar message^M                     
[21704.953609] Lustre: lustre-OST0000: haven't heard from client cd1f08cf-b9d8-4971-997a-2d05d1ce6219 (at 10.240.27.185@tcp) in 103 seconds. I think it's dead, and I am evicting it. ex      p ffff9d0d83000000, cur 1751553431 deadline 1751553428 last 1751553328^M

+1 on Lustre 2.15.7 RC1: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/e74e89c4-7136-4319-9789-519e197f819a
The failure not only occurred on sanity-sec, but also on other tests. All of them in ubuntu2204 test sessions:
lustre-b2_15-el8.10-x86_64_lustre-b2_15-ubuntu2204-x86_64-full-part-1
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/fe053d73-3e92-4e94-828f-dac3637c0849
lustre-b2_15-el8.10-x86_64_lustre-b2_15-ubuntu2204-x86_64-full-part-2
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/1562bac0-eb02-4507-9ccb-17c0c2d0d239
lustre-b2_15-el8.10-x86_64_lustre-b2_15-ubuntu2204-x86_64-full-part-3
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/913338f9-ca50-4dc3-8daf-0312e2079937
lustre-b2_15-el8.9-x86_64_lustre-b2_15-ubuntu2204-x86_64-full-part-1
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/46f5bbf5-6d6b-437c-a284-e26e64273791
lustre-b2_15-el8.9-x86_64_lustre-b2_15-ubuntu2204-x86_64-full-part-3
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/4354730b-6598-485f-91dc-96b487c286cd
+1 on master: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/566c0283-6c03-49c3-a22d-f16d337da6ee

It seems like this is a test infrastructure issue, possibly some problem with the network switch, because both the Lustre RPCs and NFS traffic suddenly stop working, and the NFS node IPs are definitely not related to the nodes in the cluster being tested.

There are a number of similar tickets (LU-18693, DCO-10365, DCO-10037, DCO-10066) where the network suddenly stops working to some client node.

+1 seen on sanity-sec test_23a: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/f17e2756-d497-4be7-9e4d-ff392b52d8e2

[21607.476016] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.42.216@tcp at host 10.240.42.216:7988
[21608.497361] LNet: 1 local NIs in recovery (showing 1): 10.240.42.217@tcp
[21608.497401] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.42.217@tcp at host 10.240.42.217:7988
[21610.545225] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.42.217@tcp at host 10.240.42.217:7988
[21968.929365] LNet: 1 local NIs in recovery (showing 1): 10.240.42.217@tcp
[21979.168792] LNet: 2 peer NIs in recovery (showing 2): 10.240.42.221@tcp, 10.240.42.218@tcp
[21989.408467] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.42.221@tcp at host 10.240.42.221:7988
[21989.410444] LNetError: Skipped 15 previous similar messages
[22027.678574] nfs: server 10.240.32.204 not responding, timed out
[22032.670375] nfs: server 10.240.32.204 not responding, timed out
:

21799.300652] nfs: server 10.240.32.204 not responding, still trying
